channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Ukraine Deploys American Autonomous Vehicles for Combat Support
item.description: Forterra, a US-based company specializing in autonomous vehicles, has deployed over 100 self-driving ATVs in conflict zones of Ukraine for nine months. These Lancer vehicles, based on Polaris ATVs wit...

item.title: ConnexOntario Faces Strain as Gambling Helpline Sees Surge in Calls Amid New Regulations
item.description: ConnexOntario, a non-profit agency managing Ontario's 24-hour gambling helpline, is overwhelmed by a surge in calls linked to the liberalization of online gambling. The organization reported a 68% inc...

item.title: Mombasa Protest Highlights Economic Struggles and Governance Concerns
item.description: Residents in Mombasa held a peaceful 15-minute march protest through the city's Central Business District after agreeing with police on a secure route. The demonstration, organized to address grievanc...

item.title: Leadership Changes at Ram Temple Trust Following Donation Theft Scandal
item.description: The trust managing India's Ram temple, built on the site of the demolished Babri Masjid and consecrated by Prime Minister Narendra Modi in 2024, has undergone leadership changes after allegations of d...

item.title: Trump to Back F-35 Jet Sale to Türkiye Amid Legal Hurdles
item.description: U.S. President Donald Trump is expected to support the potential sale of F-35 fighter jets to Türkiye during his visit to Ankara, despite unresolved legal and congressional hurdles. The decision comes...

item.title: Venezuela's earthquake aftermath: Families search for missing in rubble
item.description: Two devastating earthquakes have struck Venezuela, killing thousands and leaving families in despair as they search for loved ones trapped in the rubble. Amneiver Parra, an 18-year-old with respirator...

item.title: Joburg Ballet's Groundbreaking African Choral Ballet Reimagines Euripides' 'The Bacchae'
item.description: Joburg Ballet and the University of Johannesburg (UJ) arts & culture department have launched *The Bacchae: An African Choral Ballet*, a world-first production that reinterprets Euripides' ancient Gre...

item.title: Southern Africans still awaiting asbestos compensation from British trust despite 25 years
item.description: A report by investigative journalism website Confront reveals that millions of pounds from a British asbestos compensation fund remain unclaimed by victims in southern Africa. Despite a trust set up b...

item.title: Understanding Chrislam: A Unique Blend of Christianity and Islam in Lagos
item.description: This article explores the phenomenon of Chrislam, a syncretic religious practice blending Christianity and Islam in Lagos, Nigeria. It highlights how residents navigate rapid urbanization, economic ch...

item.title: Arizona Democrats warn Medicaid and SNAP cuts are leaving hundreds of thousands without benefits
item.description: Arizona Democrats are criticizing the long-term effects of President Donald Trump's One Big Beautiful Bill Act, arguing that the legislation has significantly reduced access to healthcare and food ass...

item.title: AI Degrees and Their Role in a Changing Job Market
item.description: The article explores the rapid expansion of artificial intelligence (AI) degree programs across universities, highlighting both opportunities and concerns. As demand for AI expertise grows, institutio...

item.title: Ramos' two home runs and 5 RBIs lead Giants to 10-1 victory over Blue Jays
item.description: In a dominant performance, New York Yankees' star player Ramos delivered a standout game with two home runs and five RBIs, propelling the Giants to a decisive 10-1 win against the Blue Jays. The game,...

item.title: Landslide in Kerala's Wayanad kills one, triggers red alert amid heavy rain
item.description: A red alert was issued in Kerala's Wayanad district after a landslide at the Kalladi tunnel project site killed one person and triggered rescue operations. The incident occurred around 11 am, promptin...

item.title: 58 Students Face Consequences for Academic Dishonesty Involving ChatGPT
item.description: Fifty-eight students have had their examination results nullified after being found guilty of academic dishonesty, including the unauthorized use of ChatGPT, in a move that highlights growing concerns...

item.title: Trump's alleged interference in World Cup match sparks global controversy
item.description: The article discusses the controversy surrounding Donald Trump's alleged interference in a World Cup match between the USA and Belgium. Trump claimed to have personally intervened in three phone calls...

item.title: Nigeria Police Hunt for Adeyemi Over Fake Agency Scandal
item.description: The Nigerian police are actively searching for Adeniyi Adeyemi, the disgraced director general of the fake agency PFIPC, following allegations of fraud and misuse of public resources. The scandal erup...

item.title: Kenya Police Stop Youth Protest Near Harambee House During Saba Saba Restrictions
item.description: Kenyan police in plain clothes arrested several youths near Harambee House and Jogoo House on Tuesday morning as they tried to gather for Saba Saba commemorations. The arrests happened before the yout...

item.title: Mike Phillips' Leadership Offers Hope for Gilas Amid Challenges
item.description: The article discusses how Mike Phillips' energetic and positive attitude is providing a spark of optimism for the Gilas Pilipinas basketball team, despite facing numerous challenges. FIBA technical di...

item.title: Public Protector Releases Findings on Cape Town Municipal Services Investigation
item.description: The Public Protector South Africa (PPSA) has concluded a systemic investigation into allegations that the City of Cape Town failed to provide basic municipal services in the communities of Langa Flats...

item.title: Damascus blasts during Macron's visit injure 18
item.description: Two bombs exploded near the hotel in Damascus where French President Emmanuel Macron held meetings during his visit to Syria, injuring 18 people including four police officers. The blasts occurred in ...
